MOTU UltraLite-mk3

Section-based notes for routing, CueMix, converters, monitoring, and hardware setup.

  • Overview
  • Hybrid FireWire/USB2
  • Mic/Guitar Inputs
  • Comprehensive I/O
  • Mixing and effects
  • CueMix FX software
  • Classic reverb
  • Modeled analog EQ
  • Vintage compression
  • Tuner & analysis tools
  • Clock and sync
  • MIDI
  • Expansion & drivers
  • Feature Summary

Feature Summary

Source: MOTU product page

Technical Specifications

FeatureSpecification
Analog Inputs10 (2 mic/inst combo XLR/TRS front + 8 balanced TRS rear)
Analog Outputs14 (10 line + 2 main + 2 phones)
Digital I/OS/PDIF stereo in/out (RCA)
MIDI1× 5-pin DIN In, 1× 5-pin DIN Out
Sample Rates44.1, 48, 88.2, 96, 176.4, 192 kHz
Bit Depth24-bit
Dynamic Range (EIN)~116 dB (mic preamps)
Computer ConnectionFireWire 400/800, hi-speed USB2
Bus PowerFireWire only (USB2 requires included DC supply)
On-board EffectsClassic Reverb™, 7-band Modeled EQ, LA-2A Leveler™, Conventional Compressor
DSP Mixer16-bus (8 stereo), 32-bit float precision
Standalone OperationYes — mix settings stored in flash memory
Control SoftwareCueMix FX (Win/Mac), AudioDesk (Mac only)
Form FactorHalf-rack, 1U rack mount possible with optional bracket

Role in This Studio

  • Central audio hub: SM58 (input 1), VT-3 (inputs 3/4), Hydrasynth (inputs 5/6)
  • Main output to Yamaha HS7 studio monitors (channels 1/2)
  • Headphone monitoring for tracking (front-panel phones output)
  • MIDI router: receives 5-pin DIN from controllers; sends to Hydrasynth
  • Latency-free monitoring via CueMix FX DSP mixer — no computer processing in the monitoring path

Built for fast lookups, cleaner routing decisions, and practical studio recall.
